what_it_does: An empty Git placeholder file that causes the otherwise empty contracts/ directory to be tracked by version control.
observations: Filename is .gitkeep, a conventional Git sentinel
It sits in the contracts/ directory
conventions: Follows the common Git convention of using a .gitkeep file to preserve an empty directory in a repository
notes: A housekeeping file whose only role is to keep the contracts directory under version control until real content is added.
questions: What kinds of contracts or contract-related artifacts are intended to live in this directory?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of a .gitkeep file is to retain an empty directory, and that matches what it does.
intention_signals: It appears to be reserving a contracts/ namespace in the repository so the directory structure is established before any contract files are created.
contribution: It preserves the contracts/ directory path in version control, ensuring downstream tooling, documentation, and file discovery can rely on the directory's existence.
summary_for_embedding: An empty .gitkeep file in company/contracts that keeps the contracts directory tracked by Git.
intention_for_embedding: To reserve and persist the contracts directory in the repository until actual contract files are added.
